---
title: "Want to help?"
subtitle: "We are looking for code that can be shared and reused across register-based research projects"
date: last-modified
---

This guide is continuously evolving - and we know that many researchers have solved the same classic problems over and over, each in their own project, without the code ever being shared.

**Do you have code lying around that solves one of these problems? We would love to hear from you.**

---

## What we are looking for

Examples of code that could benefit several of your colleagues:

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.5rem; background: #fafafa;"}
#### SEPLINE
Algorithm for identifying sepsis in LPR data - e.g. based on the SEPLINE criteria.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.5rem; background: #fafafa;"}
#### Weighted medication use
Methods for calculating exposure measures from LMDB - e.g. PDC (Proportion of Days Covered), MPR, or DIRD-based measures.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.5rem; background: #fafafa;"}
#### Family linkage
Linking parents and children via CPR number - e.g. to transfer exposures or covariates across generations.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.5rem; background: #fafafa;"}
#### Episode construction
Merging overlapping or adjacent LPR contacts into continuous hospitalisation episodes.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 8px; padding: 1.5rem; background: #fafafa;"}
#### Time-varying covariates
Constructing covariates that change over follow-up time - e.g. medication use, diagnoses, or socioeconomic status from year to year.
:::

:::

---

::: {.callout-note}
It does not need to be finished or validated - we are happy to work further with drafts. If you have something else entirely that solves a general problem in register data, you are very welcome to get in touch as well.
:::

**Contact:** [saras@clin.au.dk](mailto:saras@clin.au.dk)
